MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-25-2022 Continued To Aug-31-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar May-06-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: CROSS DEFENDANT'S OTIS ELEVATOR COMPANY's Motion for Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant OTIS ELEVATOR COMPANY's motion for summary judgment on AAA Attic Self Storage, LLC's cross-complaint is granted.

AAA cites no authority holding that Otis owed AAA a duty to warn that members of the public should not use the freight elevator. The undisputed facts show that while Otis installed the elevator in 1954, (1) elevator code requirements assume the presence of freight operators and handlers who are familiar with the elevator door operation; (2) the owner of the freight elevator (AAA) is responsible for determining who is authorized to use the elevator, for providing training instruction for those who are authorized to use the elevator, for all construction and use permits, and for ensuring compliance with all applicable elevator codes and regulations; (3) Otis did not have responsibility for training the operator of the freight elevator; and (4) the subject elevator gate operated as it was designed and it was not malfunctioning when it hit Plaintiff. (Otis' UMF 16, 19, 21, 22, 25.) Santilli v Otis Elevator Company (1989) 215 Cal.App.3d 211 does not hold that Otis owed a duty to warn AAA in such circumstances. Indeed, it is unclear whether AAA occupied the premises when the elevator was installed in 1954.

In sum, AAA fails to present evidence showing that Otis owed a duty to AAA or to plaintiff given the adduced undisputed facts. AAA provides no facts showing how Otis can be liable under Restatement 2d. Torts sec. 388. (See Stevens v. Parke, Davis & Co. (1973) 9 Cal.3d 51, 64-65 ["One who supplies a product directly or through a third person 'for another to use is subject to liability to those whom the supplier should expect to use the (product) with the consent of the other . . . for physical harm caused by the use of the (product) in the manner for which and by a person for whose use it is supplied, if the supplier ( ) (a) knows or has reason to know that the (product) is or is likely to be dangerous for the use for which it is supplied, and ( ) (b) has no reason to believe that those for whose use the (product) is supplied will realize its dangerous condition, and (c) fails to exercise reasonable care to inform them of its dangerous condition or of facts which make it likely to be dangerous." (quoting Rest. 2d 388)].)

Order signed.

Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/SRB)
